nodes.name: 南美洲
nodes.summary: 南美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联70种鸟类，包括小美洲鸵、大美洲鸵、北山䳍等70种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 鸠鸽科
nodes.summary: 鸠鸽科（Columbidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联299种鸟类，如Purple-winged ground dove、Moreno's ground dove、Black-winged ground dove等299种鸟类。

nodes.name: Ruddy pigeon
nodes.summary: Ruddy pigeon（Ruddy pigeon，学名 Patagioenas subvinacea），分类上属于鸽形目，并归入鸠鸽科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于平均海拔、南美洲、Niels Krabbe，主要威胁包括森林砍伐。

nodes.name: Niels Krabbe
nodes.summary: Niels Krabbe是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Ruddy pigeon，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 平均海拔
nodes.summary: 平均海拔是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Andean guan、鳳冠孔雀雉等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸽形目
nodes.summary: 鸽形目（Columbiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联299种鸟类，如Purple-winged ground dove、Moreno's ground dove、Black-winged ground dove等299种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: subvinacea. thumb|left In Central America it is found in highland forest canopy and semi-open woodland from 1500 m (5000 ft) ASL to the timberline

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In South America it occurs in the canopy of humid forest from near sea-level to 1500 m (5000 ft) ASL, occasionally higher; exceptionally, they may range up to 3000 m ASL or more.Krabbe, Niels; Flórez, Pablo; Suárez, Gustavo; Castaño, José; Arango, Juan David & Duque, Arley (2006) The birds of Páramo de Frontino, western Andes of Colombia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In South America it occurs in the canopy of humid forest from near sea-level to 1500 m (5000 ft) ASL, occasionally higher; exceptionally, they may range up to 3000 m ASL or more.Krabbe, Niels; Flórez, Pablo; Suárez, Gustavo; Castaño, José; Arango, Juan David & Duque, Arley (2006) The birds of Páramo de Frontino, western Andes of Colombia

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: PDF fulltext It is not uncommon across its wide range but due to projected deforestation is thus classified as a Least Concern species by the IUCN. It is normally seen in pairs as it forages in the tree tops for fruits and berries—being particularly fond of mistletoe fruit – but may occasionally be seen on tracks and roadside seeking grit
